channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: Takeaways from the AP's investigation into Osprey safety issues
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: The Osprey's safety issues spiked over five years and caused deaths. Pilots still want to fly it
item.description: The Osprey is back in the air after being grounded for months following a crash last November that killed eight U.S. service members in Japan.
item.dc:creator: Tara Copp, Kevin Vineys And Aaron Kessler, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Bear shot, killed by homeowner in Southern California
item.description: A California man is being criticized after he shot and killed a bear in his yard last week that he claimed was getting into his chicken coop.
item.dc:creator: Josh DuBose, KREX 5

item.title: Sister of ex-Marine charged in subway chokehold death testifies to his 'calm' spirit and patriotism
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The older sister of Daniel Penny, the former U.S. Marine charged with fatally choking a homeless man aboard a Manhattan subway, told jurors Monday that her brother was a “calm, soft-spirited person” with a reputation for honesty and integrity.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Speaker Johnson's 'epic' weekend with Trump shows strengths and limits of his power
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — There was House Speaker Mike Johnson walking behind President-elect Donald Trump's entourage into Saturday night's UFC fight at Madison Square Garden, his stature overcome by the enormity of the scene around him. And Johnson mugging with musicians Kid Rock and Jelly Roll.
item.dc:creator: LISA MASCARO, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 2 women tell Ethics panel Gaetz paid them for sex, attorney says
item.description: Two women testified before the House Ethics Committee that former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.) paid them to have sex, according to the attorney representing them.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Agent says GPS data puts Georgia student Laken Riley in same area as man accused in her death
item.description: ATHENS, Ga. (AP) — An FBI special agent testified Monday that electronic location data seems to place Georgia nursing student Laken Riley and the man accused of killing her in the same wooded area at the time of her death.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: MTA board approves Democrat Gov. Hochul’s massive NYC congestion pricing toll reboot
item.description: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority approved a congestion pricing program pitched by New York Gov. Kathy Hochul to charge as much as $15 for drivers to enter New York City.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NYC issues first drought warning in 22 years, pauses aqueduct repairs to bring in more water
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — New York City on Monday issued its first drought warning in 22 years after months of little rain -- and will restart the flow of drinking water from an out-of-service aqueduct as supplies run low. Dry conditions across the Northeast have been blamed for hundreds of brush fires.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Democrat mayor not welcome at funeral for slain police officer
item.description: Hundreds attended the funeral for Chicago Police Officer Enrique Martinez, and the city's mayor did not attend at the request of the officer's family members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden seeks nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton
item.description: President Joe Biden is requesting nearly $100 billion in emergency disaster aid after Hurricanes Helene and Milton, and other natural disasters.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 3 people are killed in random stabbings in New York City. A suspect is in custody
item.description: Officials say a man has carried out a string of stabbings across a swath of Manhattan that killed three people.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Man attacked by owls twice in less than a week
item.description: Owls aren't notoriously menacing creatures, but those living in the Old Shell Road area might not agree.
item.dc:creator: Asher Redd, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Christmas cards requested for nursing home residents impacted by Helene
item.description: She hopes the cards will remind them of this simple message: "You are not forgotten. You are loved, and you're valued.
item.dc:creator: Maggie Newland, NewsNation

item.title: About 20% of Americans regularly get their news from influencers on social media, report says
item.description: About one in five Americans – and a virtually identical share of Republicans and Democrats – regularly get their news from digital influencers who are more likely to be found on the social media platform X, according to a report released Monday by the Pew Research Center.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: House Ethics Committee to meet Wednesday amid debate over Gaetz report
item.description: Members of the House Ethics Committee will meet Wednesday, a source confirmed to The Hill, as the panel weighs whether it should release its report into former Rep. Matt Gaetz (R-Fla.), whom President-elect Trump plans to nominate to serve as attorney general.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: Should women be allowed to fight on the front lines? Trump's defense pick reignites debate
item.description: President Donald Trump’s nominee for defense secretary, Pete Hegseth, has reignited a debate that many thought had been long settled.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Neo-Nazi march in Ohio draws rebuke from Biden
item.description: President Joe Biden joined the chorus of state and city officials who are condemning a weekend neo-Nazi march in Columbus, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Brian Hofmann, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Student protesters face potential Trump crackdown
item.description: Those on campus have already seen stricter rules around demonstrations this academic year, but President-elect Trump, as a candidate, has threatened protesters with everything from military action to deportation.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, Fox 5

item.title: All eyes on House Ethics Committee amid controversy over Gaetz report
item.description: All eyes will be locked on the House Ethics Committee this week as it weighs whether to release its report into Matt Gaetz.
item.dc:creator: Mychael Schnell, NewsNation

item.title: The worst passwords you could have in 2024 — or any year
item.description: Don't rely on easy-to-remember passwords too much — because all of us tend to think more alike than we'd think.
item.dc:creator: Russell Falcon, ABC 4

item.title: Beyoncé performing at halftime of NFL Christmas Day game on Netflix
item.description: Beyoncé is coming to your home on Christmas — provided you have Netflix and are tuning in to the Baltimore Ravens-Houston Texans game.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 3 hurt when plane crashes near Southern California dragstrip hosting racing event
item.description: At least two parked vehicles on the ground were struck when the small plane crashed around near the dragstrip.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Federal workers brace for Trump overhaul of civil service
item.description: President-elect Trump’s plans to reshape the federal workforce would allow him to fire wide swaths of career employees and replace them with political appointees — a move that comes as he has demanded loyalty at every level of government.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, NewsNation

item.title: US immigration judges ordered 7K Mexicans deported last month
item.description: Over 7,100 Mexican nationals were ordered deported from the United States in October -- that's more than any other nationality group, according to new data.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, NewsNation

item.title: The 9 GOP senators who could derail Trump’s Cabinet picks
item.description: Trump can afford three defections within the Senate GOP conference and still get his picks confirmed.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Laken Riley's alleged killer Jose Ibarra flew from 'ground zero' of migrant crisis to Georgia
item.description: Laken Riley murder suspect Jose Ibarra will return to court Monday for the second day of his trial. Ibarra is accused of attacking and killing Riley on her run on Feb. 22.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump taps Brendan Carr to be FCC chairman
item.description: President-elect Trump is tapping Brendan Carr of the Federal Communications Commission (FCC) to lead the agency.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Union workers picket for 3rd day at Las Vegas casino with no talks slated
item.description: LAS VEGAS (AP) — Picketing continued Sunday outside a hotel-casino near the Las Vegas Strip that remained open with no talks scheduled between management and union members striking for a new contract.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Biden authorizes Ukraine to use US-supplied long-range missiles for deeper strikes inside Russia
item.description: President Joe Biden has authorized the use of U.S.-supplied long-range missiles by Ukraine to strike even deeper inside Russia, the latest easing of limitations meant to prevent the conflict from further spiraling, according to one U.S. official and three people familiar with the matter.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: An Israeli strike in Beirut kills Hezbollah's spokesman, while a strike in Gaza kills at least 30
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — A rare Israeli strike in central Beirut killed the Hezbollah militant group's chief spokesman on Sunday, while an Israeli strike in northern Gaza ’s Beit Lahiya killed at least 30 people, a hospital director there told The Associated Press.
item.dc:creator: FADI TAWIL, BASSEM MROUE and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Elon Musk is hiring for DOGE. Here's the type of people he wants to recruit
item.description: Just days after being tapped to co-lead the Department of Government Efficiency, Elon Musk is searching for "high-IQ" individuals to join the taskforce.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Jackson, KRON4

item.title: Longest-waiting child in Arkansas foster care system finds forever family
item.description: After more than 6,100 days in the Arkansas foster care system, 17-year-old Jose has now found his forever family.
item.dc:creator: Mattison Gafner, WGN 9

item.title: US students who box, skydive and help youth and asylum seekers are among 2025's Rhodes scholars
item.description: A group of 32 students from the United States have been selected to attend the University of Oxford as part of the prestigious Rhodes scholar program in the coming year among an international class representing more than 70 nations, scholarship officials announced.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN P. D. HANNON and JOHN HANNA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: How this duct-taped banana came to be worth $1 million
item.description: Walk into any supermarket and you can generally buy a banana for less than $1. But a banana duct-taped to a wall? That might sell for more than $1 million at an upcoming auction.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Trump attends UFC championship fight in New York, taking a break from Cabinet picks
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — President-elect Donald Trump walked out to a roaring standing ovation just ahead of the start of the UFC pay-per-view card at Madison Square Garden on Saturday night, combining two things close to his heart: fierce battles inside the octagon and New York City.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT and DAN GELSTON, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: No. 13 Purdue rediscovers strong post presence in 87-78 win over No. 2 Alabama
item.description: Boilermakers forward Trey Kaufman-Renn made 12 of 23 shots, scored a career-high 26 points and grabbed eight rebounds as No. 13 Purdue handed No. 2 Alabama an 87-78 defeat that was eerily reminiscent of last season's 92-86 loss to the Boilermakers in Toronto.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL MAROT, Associated Press, Fox 5
